content.claim: A 2017–2018 reform removed exemption from grading and narrative assessment for BTMN (but not SNI) students, creating a documented incentive mechanism for reclassification toward SNI, but expert opinion is sharply contested on whether this materially increased SNI numbers.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: No dedicated quantitative study isolating the causal effect of the 2017–18 BTMN reform on SNI reclassification was retrieved
Clinical and practitioner experts interviewed disagreed sharply on the reform's direction of effect (some predicted SNI increase, others predicted lower future education prospects from worse grades)
A legal-guidance source contests whether changes affecting BTMN should be attributed to SNI policy, suggesting possible conflation of two categories
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
